## Definition

The term *metabolism* refers to all of the chemical reactions which occur in our bodies. As sport and exercise scientists, we are interested in the study of *exercise metabolism*, which is primarily concerned with the chemical reactions that provide our muscles with the energy to exercise.

## Core Principles

The *Law of Conservation of Mass* states that the total mass of the reactants equals the total mass of the products (i.e. the number of atoms of each element is the same before and after the reaction). This is why, for example, two molecules of hydrogen may be required in a given reaction — to balance the atoms on both sides.

Although the total number of atoms is the same before and after a reaction, the structural arrangements of the atoms have been changed, and it is for this reason that the newly formed products have different chemical properties.

## Sub-concepts

- *Exercise metabolism*: the subset of metabolic chemical reactions that provide muscles with the energy required for exercise.

## Field-level Controversies

## 文獻分歧 / Discussion

## Practical Applications

Sport and exercise scientists apply the study of exercise metabolism to understand and support the energy demands of muscular work during exercise.
